facts: The patient's name is Larry.
The patient has back pain.
The back pain has lasted a couple of years.
The patient's primary care doctor referred them to the specialist.
The patient tried many things to alleviate the back pain.
The patient's back pain started after moving furniture.
The back pain feels like a sharp stabbing pain.
The pain is located in the back.
The pain goes down to the right leg.
The pain goes down to the big toe.
The patient has difficulty getting comfortable.
The discomfort worsens when sitting down.
The patient experiences numbness.
The numbness is associated with the back pain.
The numbness is located in the right leg.
The numbness goes down to the foot.
The patient experiences tingling.
The tingling is associated with the back pain.
The tingling is located in the right leg.
The tingling goes down to the foot.
The patient denied loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area.
The patient denied weakness.
The patient denied loss of control of the bladder or bowels.
The patient tried physical therapy in the past.
The patient was prescribed strong pain medications in the past.
The patient was considered for surgery in the past.
The patient wants to avoid surgery.
The patient tries to be active despite the back pain.
The patient has gained weight due to the back pain.
The patient used to like playing golf.
Golf is a relatively new activity for the patient.
The back pain has set back the patient's golfing.
The patient quit smoking twenty years ago.
The doctor reviewed the patient's vitals.
The patient's vitals look good.
The doctor performed a musculoskeletal back exam.
There is no overlying erythema on the patient's back.
There is no ecchymosis on the patient's back.
There is midline tenderness at the L4-L5 disk space.
There is right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion.
The supine straight leg test is positive.
The patient has decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at the L4 dermatome.
The patient's patellar and achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
The patient had an MRI.
The MRI shows disk herniation with nerve impingement.
The doctor diagnosed an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
The herniated disk is bulging out and pinching nerves.
The doctor recommended a spinal injection.
The spinal injection will contain a corticosteroid.
The corticosteroid will be injected into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
The injection should help with inflammation causing the pain.
The patient may need to have the injection repeated.
The injection can be repeated up to four times a year.
The procedure is relatively quick and common.
The patient should be out of the office within an hour after the procedure.
The doctor prescribed a corticosteroid.
The corticosteroid is for the patient's back pain.
The corticosteroid is administered via spinal injection.
The corticosteroid dose is not specified.
The corticosteroid frequency is not specified, but it can be repeated up to four times a year.
